title: UI Review Payload
description: Payload emitted by the ui-review skill, validated by the shared `validate-review` command and rendered into a summary PR comment (NOT posted as an inline PR review). It deliberately reuses the pr-review payload's `event`/`comments` field names so the same validator works unchanged, but each comment carries route/viewport/theme/screenshot context instead of diff-line anchors. `event` is fixed to COMMENT — this bot never approves.

event.description: Always 'COMMENT'. The ui-review bot is advisory-only — the workflow posts a plain PR comment, never an approving review. Constrained to a single value so the payload cannot request approval.

body.description: Concise top-level UI/UX summary in 2-4 sentences (overall assessment, surfaces reviewed). Do not restate individual comments (those go in `comments`). Must end with the '🤖 Generated with Claude' footer on its own line.

comments.description: UI/UX findings observed in the running app, one per comment. Empty array is allowed (no issues found).

body.description: Comment text. Must start with '🔴 **CRITICAL:** ', '🟡 **MODERATE:** ', or '🟢 **NIT:** '. State what is wrong, why it matters for the user, and (when known) a concrete fix.

route.description: The navigable path where the issue was observed, relative to the app root (e.g. '/experiments/1/traces'). Use '/' for the home page.

viewport.description: Viewport the issue was observed at.

theme.description: Color theme the issue was observed under.

screenshot.description: Optional. Filename (basename only) of the evidence screenshot under the screenshots directory ($AGENT_BROWSER_SCREENSHOT_DIR), e.g. 'traces-desktop.png'. Referenced from the rendered comment and uploaded as a workflow artifact.

changed_files.description: Optional. PR-changed files this finding relates to, for the reader's context.
